summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting with Trustee Hauserman absent due to a family emergency. Major items included the re-approval of a preliminary development plan (PDP) for a large residential development (Zoning Case 309) with extensive conditions, the recognition of Milford Fire Chief Mark Baer who thanked the township for lending a spare ambulance for three months, adoption of a new township logo, and the acknowledgment of retiring legal counsel Ernie Ramis along with the introduction of new attorney Brian Tribe.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda passed 2-0
Motion to approve payment of bills (May 12-25, 2021) passed 2-0
Motion to accept $200 in gift cards and a NutriBullet from CPAA for National Police Week passed 2-0
Motion to approve out-of-town training for Sergeant Collier for Standardized Field Sobriety Testing Instructor Training (9/13-9/17/21) at no cost passed 2-0
Motion to approve out-of-town training for Sergeant Collier for Sexual Assault Training (10/20-10/21/21) at $230 passed 2-0
Motion to approve out-of-town training for Sergeant Collier for Internal Investigations Training (11/2-11/3/21) at $230 passed 2-0
Motion to terminate contract with Dr. Matthew Hoffman as medical director effective June 30, 2021 passed 2-0
Motion to authorize fire/EMS chief to approve medical director contract with Dr. Charlton of Emergency Medicine Physicians Limited effective July 1, 2021 at $8,000/year passed 2-0
Motion to increase appropriations in fire/EMS fund by $1,915.48 for 2019 911 dispatch fee passed 2-0
Motion to table sale/donation of 1998 Sutphen fire engine to Scott Township to next meeting passed 2-0
Motion to approve PDP for Zoning Case 309 with extensive conditions passed 2-0
Motion to adopt new township logo passed 2-0
Motion to table executive session (Item M) to next meeting passed 2-0
action_items: Harry Holbrook to continue working with developer on remaining zoning conditions for Case 309 final development plan
Developer/applicant to submit traffic impact study to ODOT and Clermont County for Case 309
Fire Chief Pegram to finalize medical director contract with Dr. Charlton/Emergency Medicine Physicians Limited
Trustees to discuss sale vs. donation of 1998 Sutphen fire engine to Scott Township at next meeting with Trustee Hauserman present
Executive session item to be addressed at next meeting
key_amounts: $250 donation from Milford Community Firefighters Club toward Goshen firefighters union autism sensory kits
$200 in gift cards from CPAA for National Police Week
$230 cost for Sergeant Collier Sexual Assault Training
$230 cost for Sergeant Collier Internal Investigations Training
$8,000 per year ($4,000 every six months) for new medical director contract with Dr. Charlton
$1,915.48 increase in appropriations for 2019 911 dispatch fee
$12,000 offered by Scott Township for 1998 Sutphen fire engine
notable_topics: Milford Fire Chief Mark Baer thanking township for three-month loan of spare ambulance
Re-approval of Preliminary Development Plan (PDP) for Zoning Case 309 - large mixed-use residential development with 207+ single-family homes and 48 multifamily units
Community opposition to high-density residential development near Deerfield Road and SR 132
Termination of medical director Dr. Hoffman and new contract with Dr. Charlton/Emergency Medicine Physicians Limited
Adoption of new Goshen Township logo after multi-year branding effort
Recognition of retiring legal counsel Ernie Ramis and introduction of new attorney Brian Tribe
Police training approvals for Sergeant Collier
Potential sale or donation of surplus 1998 Sutphen fire engine to Scott Township (Adams County)
Goshen Lions Club Flags for Heroes fundraiser announcement for Fourth of July
attendees: Chief Mark Baer (Milford Community Fire Department)
Brian Tribe (new attorney from prosecutor's office)
Scott Huber (civil engineer, Everett Crumbing Associates)
Joe Perusa (developer - mentioned but unclear if spoke)
Jim (resident, 5786 Deerfield Road - last name unclear)
Unnamed public commenter (community member opposing development)
notes: Trustee Hauserman absent due to family death in Florida. The PDP for Case 309 was originally approved in 2019 but expired due to COVID delays and required re-approval. Significant community opposition was expressed regarding the development's density (~207+ homes on ~100 acres). Attorney for developer argued the re-approval is not a legislative act since rezoning was already completed. The development includes phases with single-family homes and 48 multifamily attached units. Multiple references to traffic, watershed, and sewer concerns being county/state jurisdiction rather than township. Milford's spare ambulance loan lasted approximately three months instead of the expected one month due to manufacturing delays.
title: Goshen Twp. BOT MTG 5.25.21
